text.title: Embedding Chunking Strategies Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every chunking config must enforce.

rule.statement: chunk_size measured in tokens (matching the embedder's tokenizer), never characters or bytes.
rule.rationale: Token budgets vary across alphabets; char-based limits overflow on CJK and underflow on Latin.

rule.statement: Overlap ≥10% of chunk_size between adjacent chunks. Zero-overlap rejected on prose corpora.
rule.rationale: Boundary-split sentences become unsearchable; overlap fixes ~5–8% recall.

rule.statement: Strategy chosen by corpus class: prose→recursive/sentence-aware; code→structural (AST/function-boundary); structured (JSON/HTML/MD)→header-aware; mixed→per-doc routing.
rule.rationale: Wrong strategy class costs 10–25 pp Recall@10.

rule.statement: Chunking-config promoted only when Recall@10 on held-out bench ≥ baseline. No override.
rule.rationale: Without a gate, "tune chunker" PRs ship recall regressions.

rule.statement: min_tokens ≥ 50 (else chunk has no information). max_tokens ≤ embedder context limit (else truncation).
rule.rationale: Tiny chunks have no signal; overlength is silently truncated.

rule.statement: New corpus OR recall regression AND bench set available → run the 5-step procedure.
No bench set OR existing chunker validated <90 days ago → skip; rely on prior validation.
